sentences.sentence: For centuries, historians and archaeologists have puzzled over the many mysteries of Stonehenge, a prehistoric monument that took an estimated 1,500 years to erect.
sentences.translation: 几个世纪以来，历史学家和考古学家一直对巨石阵的众多谜团感到困惑；巨石阵是一座史前遗迹，据估计耗费了1500年才建成。

structure.text: a prehistoric monument that took an estimated 1,500 years to erect

difficult_points: For centuries 与现在完成时 have puzzled over 搭配，强调困惑持续了很长时间。
a prehistoric monument 是 Stonehenge 的同位语，补充说明它的性质。
that took an estimated 1,500 years to erect 是定语从句，修饰 monument。

sentences.sentence: Located on Salisbury Plain in southern England, it is comprised of roughly 100 massive upright stones placed in a circular layout.
sentences.translation: 它位于英格兰南部的索尔兹伯里平原，由大约100块巨大的直立石块组成，这些石块呈圆形布局。

structure.text: Located on Salisbury Plain in southern England

difficult_points: Located... 是过去分词短语，说明 it 的位置。
it 指上一句的 Stonehenge。
placed in a circular layout 修饰 stones，说明石块排列方式。

sentences.sentence: Archaeologists believe England's most iconic prehistoric ruin was built in several stages, with the earliest constructed 5,000 or more years ago.
sentences.translation: 考古学家认为，英格兰最具标志性的史前遗迹是分几个阶段建成的，最早的部分建于5000多年前。

structure.text: England's most iconic prehistoric ruin was built in several stages
with the earliest constructed 5,000 or more years ago

difficult_points: believe 后省略 that，引出宾语从句。
was built 是被动语态，说明遗迹被建造的过程。
with the earliest constructed... 是 with 复合结构，补充最早阶段的时间。

sentences.sentence: First, Neolithic* Britons used primitive tools, which may have been fashioned out of deer antlers, to dig a massive circular ditch and bank, or henge.
sentences.translation: 首先，新石器时代的英国人使用原始工具挖出一个巨大的圆形沟渠和堤岸，也就是所谓的 henge；这些工具可能是用鹿角制成的。

structure.text: which may have been fashioned out of deer antlers
to dig a massive circular ditch and bank, or henge

difficult_points: which 引导非限制性定语从句，补充说明 primitive tools 的可能材质。
may have been fashioned out of 是情态动词加完成式被动，表示对过去工具来源的推测。
or henge 是对 a massive circular ditch and bank 的补充命名。

sentences.sentence: Deep pits dating back to that era and located within the circle may have once held a ring of timber posts, according to some scholars.
sentences.translation: 一些学者认为，圆圈内部那些可追溯到那个时代的深坑，可能曾经容纳过一圈木柱。

structure.text: Deep pits dating back to that era and located within the circle

difficult_points: 主语 Deep pits 后连续接两个非谓语短语，分别说明年代和位置。
may have once held 表示对过去情况的推测。
according to some scholars 表明这一说法来自部分学者，并非确定事实。

sentences.sentence: Several hundred years later, it is thought, Stonehenge's builders hoisted an estimated 80 bluestones, 43 of which remain today, into standing positions and placed them in either a horseshoe or circular formation.
sentences.translation: 人们认为，几百年后，巨石阵的建造者竖起了约80块蓝石，其中43块保存至今，并把它们排成马蹄形或圆形。
difficult_points: it is thought 是插入语，表示这是推测或学界观点。
43 of which remain today 是非限制性定语从句，which 指 80 bluestones。
hoisted...into standing positions and placed them... 是并列动作，说明竖起并排列石块。

sentences.sentence: These stones have been traced all the way to the Preseli Hills in Wales, some 300 kilometres from Stonehenge.
sentences.translation: 这些石头的来源一路追溯到了威尔士的普雷塞利山，那里距离巨石阵约300公里。

structure.text: all the way to the Preseli Hills in Wales

difficult_points: have been traced 是现在完成时被动语态，表示来源已被追溯出来。
all the way to 强调距离远，说明石头来源不在附近。
some 300 kilometres from Stonehenge 补充说明 Preseli Hills 与巨石阵的距离。

sentences.sentence: How, then, did prehistoric builders without sophisticated tools or engineering haul these boulders, which weigh up to four tons, over such a great distance?
sentences.translation: 那么，没有先进工具或工程技术的史前建造者，是如何把这些重达四吨的巨石拖过如此遥远的距离的呢？

structure.text: did prehistoric builders without sophisticated tools or engineering haul these boulders
which weigh up to four tons

difficult_points: How did...haul...? 是特殊疑问句，核心疑问是运输方式。
without sophisticated tools or engineering 修饰 builders，强调技术条件有限。
which weigh up to four tons 是非限制性定语从句，说明 boulders 的重量。

sentences.sentence: According to one long-standing theory among archaeologists, Stonehenge's builders fashioned sledges and rollers out of tree trunks to lug the bluestones from the Preseli Hills.
sentences.translation: 根据考古学界一种长期存在的理论，巨石阵的建造者用树干制作雪橇和滚轴，把蓝石从普雷塞利山拖运过来。

structure.text: According to one long-standing theory among archaeologists
fashioned sledges and rollers out of tree trunks
to lug the bluestones from the Preseli Hills

difficult_points: According to... 标明该运输方案是一种理论。
fashioned...out of tree trunks 表示用树干制作运输工具。
to lug... 是目的状语，说明制作雪橇和滚轴的用途。

sentences.sentence: They then transferred the boulders onto rafts and floated them first along the Welsh coast and then up the River Avon toward Salisbury Plain; alternatively, they may have towed each stone with a fleet of vessels.
sentences.translation: 然后，他们把巨石转移到木筏上，先沿威尔士海岸漂运，再沿埃文河上行驶向索尔兹伯里平原；另一种可能是，他们用一队船只拖运每块石头。

structure.text: They then transferred the boulders onto rafts
and floated them first along the Welsh coast and then up the River Avon toward Salisbury Plain
first along the Welsh coast and then up the River Avon
they may have towed each stone with a fleet of vessels

difficult_points: They 指上一句的 Stonehenge's builders。
first...and then... 表示运输路线的先后顺序。
alternatively 引出另一种运输假说，may have towed 表示对过去行为的推测。

sentences.sentence: More recent archaeological hypotheses have them transporting the bluestones with supersized wicker baskets on a combination of ball bearings and long grooved planks, hauled by oxen.
sentences.translation: 较新的考古假说则认为，他们用超大的柳条篮装载蓝石，借助滚珠轴承和长槽木板的组合，并由牛牵引来运输。

structure.text: on a combination of ball bearings and long grooved planks

difficult_points: have them transporting... 表示这些假说认为他们以这种方式运输。
with 与 on 两个介词短语分别说明工具和承载方式。
hauled by oxen 是过去分词短语，说明运输动力来自牛。

sentences.sentence: As early as the 1970s, geologists have been adding their voices to the debate over how Stonehenge came into being.
sentences.translation: 早在20世纪70年代，地质学家就一直在加入关于巨石阵如何形成的争论。

structure.text: to the debate over how Stonehenge came into being

difficult_points: As early as 强调地质学家参与讨论的时间很早。
have been adding 是现在完成进行时，强调从过去持续到现在的参与。
how 引导名词性从句，作 debate over 的宾语。

expressions.meaning_zh: 加入讨论；发表意见支持或参与

sentences.sentence: Challenging the classic image of industrious builders pushing, carting, rolling or hauling giant stones from faraway Wales, some scientists have suggested that it was glaciers, not humans, that carried the bluestones to Salisbury Plain.
sentences.translation: 一些科学家挑战了勤劳建造者从遥远的威尔士推、运、滚或拖巨石的传统形象，提出把蓝石带到索尔兹伯里平原的是冰川，而不是人类。

structure.text: Challenging the classic image of industrious builders pushing, carting, rolling or hauling giant stones from faraway Wales
that it was glaciers, not humans, that carried the bluestones to Salisbury Plain
it was glaciers, not humans, that carried the bluestones to Salisbury Plain

difficult_points: Challenging... 是现在分词短语，表示这些科学家的观点在挑战传统图景。
pushing, carting, rolling or hauling 是并列动名词，描述传统想象中的运输方式。
it was glaciers, not humans, that... 是强调句，强调真正搬运者可能是冰川。

sentences.sentence: Most archaeologists have remained sceptical about this theory, however, wondering how the forces of nature could possibly have delivered the exact number of stones needed to complete the circle.
sentences.translation: 然而，大多数考古学家仍对这一理论持怀疑态度，疑惑自然力量怎么可能刚好送来完成圆环所需数量的石头。

structure.text: have remained sceptical about this theory
wondering how the forces of nature could possibly have delivered the exact number of stones needed to complete the circle

difficult_points: however 位于句中，表示与前一句科学家的冰川理论形成转折。
wondering... 是现在分词短语，说明考古学家怀疑的原因。
needed to complete the circle 修饰 stones，说明所需石头数量必须刚好符合建造要求。

sentences.sentence: The third phase of construction took place around 2000 BCE.
sentences.translation: 第三阶段的建设发生在约公元前2000年。
difficult_points: The third phase 表明文章按建造阶段推进叙述。
took place 表示“发生、进行”，用于说明建设阶段的时间。
around 2000 BCE 是大约时间，不是精确年份。

sentences.sentence: At this point, sandstone slabs – known as 'sarsens' – were arranged into an outer crescent or ring; some were assembled into the iconic three-piece structures called trilithons that stand tall in the centre of Stonehenge.
sentences.translation: 此时，被称为“砂岩巨石”的砂岩板被排列成外层的新月形或环形；其中一些被组装成标志性的三石结构，即高耸在巨石阵中心的 trilithons。

structure.text: were arranged into an outer crescent or ring
some were assembled into the iconic three-piece structures called trilithons that stand tall in the centre of Stonehenge

difficult_points: 破折号中的 known as 'sarsens' 补充说明 sandstone slabs 的名称。
were arranged 和 were assembled 都是被动语态，说明石块被排列和组装。
that stand tall... 是定语从句，修饰 trilithons。

sentences.sentence: Some 50 of these stones are now visible on the site, which may once have contained many more.
sentences.translation: 这些石头中如今约有50块在遗址上可见，而该遗址过去可能曾有更多石头。

structure.text: which may once have contained many more

difficult_points: Some 50 表示“大约50块”，不是 some 的泛指用法。
which 引导非限制性定语从句，指代 the site。
may once have contained 表示对遗址过去状态的推测。

sentences.sentence: Radiocarbon dating has revealed that work continued at Stonehenge until roughly 1600 BCE, with the bluestones in particular being repositioned multiple times.
sentences.translation: 放射性碳测年显示，巨石阵的工程一直持续到约公元前1600年，尤其是蓝石曾多次被重新摆放。

structure.text: that work continued at Stonehenge until roughly 1600 BCE
with the bluestones in particular being repositioned multiple times

difficult_points: that 引导 has revealed 的宾语从句，给出测年的发现。
until roughly 1600 BCE 表示工程持续到的大致时间点。
with...being repositioned 是 with 复合结构，强调蓝石多次被移动。

sentences.sentence: But who were the builders of Stonehenge?
sentences.translation: 但是，巨石阵的建造者是谁呢？
difficult_points: But 表示文章从建造方式转向建造者身份这一新问题。
who were... 是特殊疑问句，询问主语身份。
the builders of Stonehenge 指参与不同阶段建造的人群。

sentences.sentence: In the 17th century, archaeologist John Aubrey made the claim that Stonehenge was the work of druids, who had important religious, judicial and political roles in Celtic** society.
sentences.translation: 在17世纪，考古学家约翰·奥布里提出说，巨石阵是德鲁伊的作品；德鲁伊在凯尔特社会中承担重要的宗教、司法和政治角色。

structure.text: that Stonehenge was the work of druids
who had important religious, judicial and political roles in Celtic** society

difficult_points: that 引导同位语从句，解释 claim 的具体内容。
who 引导非限制性定语从句，补充说明 druids 的社会角色。
religious, judicial and political 是并列形容词，共同修饰 roles。

sentences.sentence: This theory was widely popularized by the antiquarian William Stukeley, who had unearthed primitive graves at the site.
sentences.translation: 这一理论由古物研究者威廉·斯图克利广泛传播，他曾在该遗址发掘出原始墓葬。

structure.text: who had unearthed primitive graves at the site

difficult_points: This theory 指上一句 John Aubrey 提出的德鲁伊建造说。
was widely popularized 是被动语态，说明理论被广泛传播。
who 引导非限制性定语从句，补充 William Stukeley 的发现。

sentences.sentence: Even today, people who identify as modern druids continue to gather at Stonehenge for the summer solstice.
sentences.translation: 即使在今天，自认为是现代德鲁伊的人仍会在夏至时聚集到巨石阵。

structure.text: people who identify as modern druids

difficult_points: Even today 强调该传统或认同延续到现代。
who identify as modern druids 是定语从句，修饰 people。
for the summer solstice 表示聚集的时间或目的与夏至有关。

sentences.sentence: However, in the mid-20th century, radiocarbon dating demonstrated that Stonehenge stood more than 1,000 years before the Celts inhabited the region.
sentences.translation: 然而，在20世纪中期，放射性碳测年证明，巨石阵早在凯尔特人居住该地区之前1000多年就已经存在。

structure.text: that Stonehenge stood more than 1,000 years before the Celts inhabited the region

difficult_points: However 引出证据对德鲁伊建造说的反驳。
that 引导 demonstrated 的宾语从句，说明测年结果。
before the Celts inhabited the region 是时间状语从句，表明巨石阵比凯尔特人早得多。

sentences.sentence: Many modern historians and archaeologists now agree that several distinct tribes of people contributed to Stonehenge, each undertaking a different phase of its construction.
sentences.translation: 如今，许多现代历史学家和考古学家一致认为，几个不同的部落都对巨石阵作出了贡献，每个部落承担了不同的建造阶段。

structure.text: that several distinct tribes of people contributed to Stonehenge
each undertaking a different phase of its construction
a different phase of its construction

difficult_points: that 引导 agree 的宾语从句，说明现代学者较一致的观点。
contributed to Stonehenge 表示参与或促成巨石阵的建造。
each undertaking... 是独立主格结构，说明不同部落分别承担不同阶段。

sentences.sentence: Bones, tools and other artefacts found on the site seem to support this hypothesis.
sentences.translation: 在遗址上发现的骨骼、工具和其他人工制品似乎支持这一假说。

structure.text: Bones, tools and other artefacts found on the site

difficult_points: found on the site 是过去分词短语，修饰 Bones, tools and other artefacts。
seem to support 表示“似乎支持”，语气保留不确定性。
this hypothesis 指上一句多个不同部落参与建造的观点。

sentences.sentence: The first stage was achieved by Neolithic agrarians who were likely to have been indigenous to the British Isles.
sentences.translation: 第一阶段由新石器时代的农耕者完成，他们很可能是英伦群岛的原住民。

structure.text: who were likely to have been indigenous to the British Isles
to have been indigenous to the British Isles

difficult_points: was achieved 是被动语态，by 短语说明完成者。
who 引导定语从句，修饰 Neolithic agrarians。
were likely to have been 表示对过去身份的推测。

sentences.sentence: Later, it is believed, groups with advanced tools and a more communal way of life left their mark on the site.
sentences.translation: 后来，人们认为，拥有先进工具和更 communal 的生活方式的群体在该遗址上留下了他们的印记。

structure.text: groups with advanced tools and a more communal way of life

difficult_points: it is believed 是插入语，表示这一说法是学界推测。
with advanced tools and a more communal way of life 修饰 groups，说明这些群体特征。
left their mark 是比喻表达，表示对遗址建造产生影响。

sentences.sentence: Some believe that they were immigrants from the European continent, while others maintain that they were probably native Britons, descended from the original builders.
sentences.translation: 有些人认为他们是来自欧洲大陆的移民，而另一些人则坚持认为他们可能是本土英国人，是最初建造者的后代。

structure.text: that they were immigrants from the European continent
that they were probably native Britons

difficult_points: Some...while others... 对比两种关于后来建造者来源的观点。
两个 that 分别引导 believe 和 maintain 的宾语从句。
descended from the original builders 修饰 native Britons，说明其血缘来源。

sentences.sentence: If the facts surrounding the architects and construction of Stonehenge remain shadowy at best, the purpose of the striking monument is even more of a mystery.
sentences.translation: 如果说围绕巨石阵设计者和建造过程的事实充其量仍然模糊不清，那么这座醒目遗迹的用途就更加神秘了。

structure.text: If the facts surrounding the architects and construction of Stonehenge remain shadowy at best
the facts surrounding the architects and construction of Stonehenge
surrounding the architects and construction of Stonehenge
the purpose of the striking monument
is even more of a mystery

difficult_points: If 引导条件状语从句，这里有“即便/如果说”的让步意味。
surrounding... 是现在分词短语，修饰 the facts。
even more of a mystery 表示用途比建造者和建造过程更加神秘。

sentences.sentence: While there is consensus among the majority of modern scholars that Stonehenge once served the function of burial ground, they have yet to determine what other purposes it had.
sentences.translation: 虽然大多数现代学者一致认为巨石阵曾经作为墓地使用，但他们尚未确定它还有哪些其他用途。

structure.text: While there is consensus among the majority of modern scholars that Stonehenge once served the function of burial ground
there is consensus among the majority of modern scholars
that Stonehenge once served the function of burial ground

difficult_points: While 引导让步状语从句，承认已有共识后转向未解问题。
that 引导同位语从句，解释 consensus 的内容。
have yet to determine 表示“仍未确定”。

sentences.sentence: In the 1960s, the astronomer Gerald Hawkins suggested that the cluster of megalithic stones operated as a form of calendar, with different points corresponding to astrological phenomena such as solstices, equinoxes and eclipses occurring at different times of the year.
sentences.translation: 20世纪60年代，天文学家杰拉尔德·霍金斯提出，这组巨石像一种日历一样运作，不同的位置对应一年中不同时间出现的天文现象，如至日、昼夜平分点和日月食。

structure.text: that the cluster of megalithic stones operated as a form of calendar
with different points corresponding to astrological phenomena such as solstices, equinoxes and eclipses occurring at different times of the year

difficult_points: that 引导 suggested 的宾语从句，说明霍金斯提出的观点。
with different points corresponding to... 是 with 复合结构，解释日历功能如何体现。
occurring at different times of the year 修饰 solstices, equinoxes and eclipses，说明这些现象出现的时间。

expressions.text: at different times of the year

sentences.sentence: While his theory has received a considerable amount of attention over the decades, critics maintain that Stonehenge's builders probably lacked the knowledge necessary to predict such events or that England's dense cloud cover would have obscured their view of the skies.
sentences.translation: 尽管几十年来他的理论受到了相当多的关注，批评者仍坚持认为，巨石阵的建造者可能缺乏预测这类事件所需的知识，或者英格兰厚密的云层会遮挡他们观察天空的视野。

structure.text: While his theory has received a considerable amount of attention over the decades
that Stonehenge's builders probably lacked the knowledge necessary to predict such events
or that England's dense cloud cover would have obscured their view of the skies

difficult_points: While 引导让步状语从句，承认理论受关注但引出批评。
maintain 后接两个由 that 引导、or 连接的宾语从句。
would have obscured 表示对过去可能情况的推测。

expressions.text: receive a considerable amount of attention

sentences.sentence: More recently, signs of illness and injury in the human remains unearthed at Stonehenge led a group of British archaeologists to speculate that it was considered a place of healing, perhaps because bluestones were thought to have curative powers.
sentences.translation: 更近一些时候，在巨石阵出土的人类遗骸中发现的疾病和伤害迹象，促使一组英国考古学家推测，它曾被视为一个疗愈之地，或许是因为人们认为蓝石具有治疗力量。

structure.text: signs of illness and injury in the human remains unearthed at Stonehenge
led a group of British archaeologists to speculate
that it was considered a place of healing
because bluestones were thought to have curative powers

difficult_points: 主语较长，核心是 signs，后面的介词短语和分词短语说明这些迹象在哪里发现。
led...to speculate 表示这些迹象促使考古学家作出推测。
because 引导原因状语从句，解释为何巨石阵可能被视为疗愈之地。
